texto: I've always had a career. I have been working hard since I was 15 years old. Being someone's 'girlfriend' was never what I wanted to be famous for. What makes you 'famous' isn't always what you want to be 'labeled' as or known for.
explicacao: Citação de Adrienne Bailon sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “I've always had a career. I have been working hard since I was 15 years old. Being someone's 'gir…” articula trabalho, working-hard, want em registro de trabalho, com relevância para contextos como termino e trabalho.

frase_original: I've always had a career. I have been working hard since I was 15 years old. Being someone's 'girlfriend' was never what I wanted to be famous for. What makes you 'famous' isn't always what you want to be 'labeled' as or known for.

texto: I'm very open and honest, and I feel like it's important for women to hear that we're not perfect; none of us are perfect. And sometimes we aspire to have these ideas in our heads of what perfection is, and I probably would've been better off if I'd left myself alone.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Adrienne Bailon: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “I'm very open and honest, and I feel like it's important for women to hear that we're not perfect…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que autoestima, women, myself pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de myself e perfection.

frase_original: I'm very open and honest, and I feel like it's important for women to hear that we're not perfect; none of us are perfect. And sometimes we aspire to have these ideas in our heads of what perfection is, and I probably would've been better off if I'd left myself alone.
